solution: 
```python
import heapq
from typing import List

class KthLargest:

    def __init__(self, k: int, nums: List[int]):
        self.k = k
        self.heap = nums
        heapq.heapify(self.heap)
        while len(self.heap) > k:
            heapq.heappop(self.heap)

    def add(self, val: int) -> int:
        heapq.heappush(self.heap, val)
        if len(self.heap) > self.k:
            heapq.heappop(self.heap)
        return self.heap[0]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N log K)
space complexity: O(K)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a min-heap to maintain the k largest elements. When a new score is added, it is pushed into the heap. If the size of the heap exceeds k, the smallest element (the root of the min-heap) is removed. This ensures that the heap always contains the k largest elements, with the root being the kth largest. The main strength is the efficient handling of insertions and maintaining the kth largest element in logarithmic time. The weakness is that it only keeps the top k elements, which may not be suitable for scenarios requiring access to all elements.
